data.task:
DIAGNOSTIC ATOM (read-mostly; produce one artifact). Goal: build the owner map + evidence pack for the repeated founder-brief format drift, so a later atom can fix the real owner instead of patching one prompt. Do NOT change any product behavior in this atom.

WHAT TO PRODUCE: write a single markdown diagnostic to `cocoder/priorities/founder-brief-durability-ownermap.md` containing:
  1. EVIDENCE PACK — the six observed founder-brief format mismatches. For each: a one-line description, the exact file:line(s) that emitted/asserted the format, and the commit or run artifact that shows the drift (search `cocoder/SESSION_LOG.md`, `cocoder/SESSION_LOG_ARCHIVE.md`, `cocoder/failure-catalog.md`, and run artifacts for the actual occurrences — do NOT invent; if you can only confirm fewer than six concrete occurrences, list what you found and say so explicitly).
  2. OWNER MAP — every source that can shape the founder brief / wrap / closeout / equivalent Play output. Enumerate concretely with file paths: the wrap-up Play (`packages/personas/base/plays/wrap-up.md`), any persona prompts that emit closeout/brief text, the runner/status projection surfaces, daemon/UI text, stored pickup briefs, and any tests/fixtures that pin the format. For each surface, state whether it DEFINES the format, DERIVES from another owner, or DUPLICATES it.
  3. SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH — name the one owner that SHOULD own the founder-brief format, and list every competing/duplicate contract that must be aligned or removed.
  4. TEST GAP — state where a test/fixture must live so an old format reappearing FAILS, and whether such a test exists today.
  5. TICKET REVIEW — review and summarize, with fold-in disposition (folded / sibling / closed-by-repair) and why: `cocoder/tickets/open/0005-persona-file-memory-migrations.md` (treat as direct evidence — unmigrated persona/shared-standards lessons, governed-file-vs-side-channel-memory failure mode), `cocoder/tickets/open/0012-design-ref-rebuild-clobber-guard.md`, `cocoder/tickets/open/0015-tickets-silently-dropped-without-frontmatter.md`, `cocoder/tickets/closed/0008-post-wrap-founder-interaction-contract.md`. Also scan `cocoder/tickets/open/**` for any other ticket about source-of-truth drift, authoring-format enforcement, founder brief/wrap/closeout, Play output, persona/standards memory, or generated-artifact clobbering, and list them.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: the doc exists at the path above; every claimed mismatch and owner cites a real file:line you actually opened (no fabricated citations); the four named tickets each have an explicit disposition; the doc ends with a proposed single owner and the smallest set of aligns/removals + the one test that would enforce it. WHAT MUST NOT BREAK: change nothing outside `cocoder/priorities/founder-brief-durability-ownermap.md` — this atom is diagnosis only, no edits to prompts, Plays, runner, or tests.

data.reason: Owner map is excellent but breached the diagnosis-only boundary: the runner/test fix was committed out-of-band (90599db, bypassing this gate) while the required doc is left uncommitted and its 'Proposed Repair' misrepresents that shipped repair as future work.

data.task:
DOCUMENTATION CONSOLIDATION ATOM (one file only; no product code). Context: the single-source-of-truth repair already SHIPPED and is verified green in commit 90599db (runner now derives the founder-closeout contract from the wrap-up Play's fenced block; end-to-end label-rename test passes; 110 tests green). Do NOT touch runner.ts, tests, prompts, Plays, or any product code — that work is done and gated. Do NOT create a second durability doc (no new file under cocoder/priorities/) — we keep ONE owner.

GOAL: complete the priority's evidence requirement by enriching the ONE existing committed doc `docs/founder-brief-format-durability.md` so it carries the EVIDENCE PACK of the six OBSERVED real founder-brief format-drift occurrences (the backward-looking proof that the drift actually happened and motivated the shipped repair) — which the doc currently lacks (it only lists forward-looking enforcement-rule classes).

WHAT TO ADD/CHANGE in `docs/founder-brief-format-durability.md` ONLY:
  1. Add a new section `## Evidence pack — six observed occurrences` listing up to six real, distinct founder-brief / closeout-format drift occurrences that actually happened. For EACH occurrence: a one-line description and an exact file:line citation you ACTUALLY OPENED AND CONFIRMED. PRIMARY evidence must be DURABLE COMMITTED records: `cocoder/failure-catalog.md` (the F18/F19/F21 entries about checklist-instead-of-runnable next action, false 'verified for landing' claims, and the design-ref/handoff drift), `cocoder/SESSION_LOG.md`, and `cocoder/SESSION_LOG_ARCHIVE.md`. You MAY additionally cite run artifacts under `local/runs/<run>/` (e.g. wrapup-out.txt, pickup.md, record.md, deb-status.md) ONLY IF that exact file still exists on disk — verify each path before citing it; if it does not exist, cite the durable committed record instead. CRITICAL: do not invent citations. If you can confirm fewer than six distinct occurrences from real files, list only the ones you confirmed and state plainly how many were confirmable and why the rest could not be.
  2. Rename/reframe the existing `## Six observed mismatch classes` section to make clear it is the ENFORCEMENT-RULE view (the format rules the runner now derives and enforces), distinct from the evidence pack of historical occurrences in step 1. Both may stay, but they must not be confusable as the same list.
  3. Add one line near the top noting the repair SHIPPED in commit 90599db so the doc reflects current repo state (it is the diagnosis-of-record for an already-completed fix, not a proposal of future work).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: `docs/founder-brief-format-durability.md` contains (a) an evidence pack of confirmed real observed occurrences, each with a file:line citation to a file you actually opened, with an explicit count/honesty note if fewer than six; (b) the existing owner map, ticket dispositions (0005/0008/0012/0015), single-source-of-truth statement, 'why it drifted' reason, and architecture rule all preserved; (c) the enforcement-rule-classes section clearly distinguished from the evidence pack; (d) a note that the repair shipped in 90599db. WHAT MUST NOT BREAK: change NOTHING outside `docs/founder-brief-format-durability.md`. Do NOT run git, do NOT commit, do NOT create any other file — leave your change in the working tree for the verify gate to commit on pass.

data.reason: Docs-only, scope clean; evidence pack of six real observed occurrences added with citations I verified against actual failure-catalog F18/F19/F20/F21 and run_75/76/77/78/79/94 artifacts; enforcement classes distinguished and repair correctly marked shipped in 90599db.

data.outcome: ✅ COMMITTED on `main` — 3 commit(s) on the active branch (no landing step; work is on the branch by construction). Committed out-of-lane (flagged, NOT held back): docs/founder-brief-format-durability.md.
